title: Recording bundle configuration (record.yaml)
description: Formal shape of record-config.yaml — the per-bundle recording configuration an adopter writes for a CLI-demo bundle: working directory, the two iTerm2 window rectangles, output directory, optional font knobs, and the optional after_record hook list. Document-shaped schema: the file IS the resource (no top-level id collection). Models the recording configuration only, not the storyboard document (per demo-recording:DEC-001). Companion per COR-018 / the .pkit/schemas/ area conventions.

pkit_schema.description: Self-describing adopter-data binding (per COR-023). Expected value: 'demo-recording:record-config'.

binds_to.description: Per-schema fallback binding (per COR-023). Present on the capability-side schema YAML so the resolver can match adopter record.yaml files without an explicit `pkit_schema:` field.

cwd.description: Absolute path the RECORDING and CONTROL windows cd into before the demo runs — the directory the demo drives.

windows.description: The two iTerm2 window rectangles, in macOS {left, top, right, bottom} pixel bounds.

recording.description: Bounds of the RECORDING window (visible in the video).

control.description: Bounds of the CONTROL window (off the recording region; drives the take).

recordings_dir.description: Where video files land. Relative paths resolve against record.yaml's directory; absolute paths are used as-is. Defaults to ./recordings when omitted.

font_size.description: iTerm font size for the demo windows (readable for video). Defaults to 18 when omitted.

font_name.description: iTerm font family (Core Text PostScript name). Defaults to Menlo-Regular when omitted.

hooks.description: Optional post-recording hook scripts (per the after_record contract in the capability README).

after_record.description: Scripts run sequentially, in order, after a successful recording. Relative paths resolve against record.yaml's directory. Each must be executable.

window_bounds.description: iTerm2 window bounds as [left, top, right, bottom] pixel coordinates.
